title: refund — marketing events (negative-value conversions)
description: Marketing-lane events fired by the refund module — every RefundSucceeded fires a downstream marketing::RefundIssued NEGATIVE-VALUE CONVERSION event. Ad networks subtract the refund_amount from the ad campaign's revenue attribution (ROAS adjustment) + retrain fraud models on refunds correlated with specific ad clicks + adjust bidding on customer segments with high refund rates. Payload includes the original conversion's ad_network + campaign_id (looked up via the payment's linked marketing_event) so the ad network can attribute the reversal to the correct conversion. Consent tier is `advertising` — customers who have withdrawn advertising consent have their refund reversals suppressed at dispatch. Refund still succeeds; only the marketing lane skips.

events.name: Refund Issued
properties: reversed_amount_cents (same as amount_cents; explicit for provider APIs that require this shape)
events.description: Fires on RefundSucceeded. Distinct from the coupon module's 'Referral Coupon Redemption Reversed' event — that fires on coupon-redemption reversal (clawback of a discount); this fires on payment reversal (clawback of the whole purchase). Both may fire for the same refund when the refunded invoice both used a coupon AND was linked to an ad-network conversion event.

events.name: Refund Issued Partial
properties: amount_cents (positive — the refunded portion, NOT the full purchase)
original_conversion_amount_cents (payment.amount_cents — for provider APIs that reason about proportional reversal)
events.description: Distinct from 'Refund Issued' for the partial-refund case. Some ad networks (Meta CAPI, Google Ads EC) accept partial-reversal payloads with the original conversion amount + the reversal amount; other networks (TikTok, Microsoft, Snapchat) only accept full reversals — for those, the refund module dispatches nothing on partial refunds (accepting the ROAS overcounting) UNLESS the partial amount >= 50% of the original (then dispatches as full reversal). Configured per-provider in marketing module.

delivery_semantics.retry_policy: exponential backoff (10s / 60s / 5m / 30m / 3h / 24h) up to 6 attempts; then dead-letter queue for manual replay
delivery_semantics.circuit_breaker: per-provider circuit breaker in the marketing module; if one provider is down, the others still receive events
delivery_semantics.ledger: growth::marketing owns the two-table ledger: marketing_events (canonical) + marketing_deliveries (per-provider-per-attempt log)

pii_hashing.user_id: sent as external_id (unhashed) — matches ad-network's advanced-matching contract

notes: This is the MARKETING lane per growth-and-observability.md — different consent tier (`advertising`, subset of marketing) than the analytics lane (`essential` for RefundSucceeded).
Ad networks require SHA-256 hashing of PII per their contracts — the growth::marketing module handles hashing, this module just declares which properties are PII.
Reversal events are load-bearing — without them, ROAS calculations inflate + the ad network keeps bidding on false conversions + retrains fraud models on non-existent purchases.

The marketing event lookup uses payment.metadata.marketing_conversion_event_id (populated when the parent payment fired a conversion event at capture time). Payments without a linked marketing conversion event do NOT fire the marketing lane — the refund still succeeds internally.

Consent-gate: `advertising` tier. Customers who have withdrawn advertising consent (via consent module) have their refund marketing events suppressed at dispatch. The refund itself still succeeds; only the marketing lane skips.

For coupon-cascaded reversals, the coupon module fires its OWN marketing reversal event ('Referral Coupon Redemption Reversed') — the two events are distinct + additive at the ad-network side. Meta's dedup is via event_id in the payload; the marketing module ensures unique event_ids per module + refund + coupon.
